2604.12919.title: MetFuse: Figurative Fusion between Metonymy and Metaphor
2604.12919.paper_summary: Metonymy and metaphor often co-occur in natural language, yet computational work has studied them largely in isolation. We introduce a framework that transforms a literal sentence into three figurative variants: metonymic, metaphoric, and hybrid. Using this framework, we construct MetFuse, the first dedicated dataset of figurative fusion between metonymy and metaphor, containing 1,000 human-verified meaning-aligned quadruplets totaling 4,000 sentences. Extrinsic experiments on eight existing benchmarks show that augmenting training data with MetFuse consistently improves both metonymy and metaphor classification, with hybrid examples yielding the largest gains on metonymy tasks. Using this dataset, we also analyze how the presence of one figurative type impacts another. Our findings show that both human annotators and large language models better identify metonymy in hybrid sentences than in metonymy-only sentences, demonstrating that the presence of a metaphor makes a metonymic noun more explicit. Our dataset is publicly available at: https://github.com/cincynlp/MetFuse.
2604.12919.paper_summary_zh: 轉喻和隱喻在自然語言中經常同時出現，但計算工作大多是孤立地研究它們。我們介紹了一個框架，將字面句子轉換為三種比喻變體：轉喻、隱喻和混合。利用這個框架，我們構建了MetFuse，首個專門針對轉喻和隱喻之間比喻融合的數據集，包含1,000個經過人類驗證的意義對齊四元組，共計4,000個句子。對八個現有基準的外部實驗顯示，使用MetFuse增強訓練數據能持續改善轉喻和隱喻的分類，其中混合示例在轉喻任務上獲得了最大的增益。利用這個數據集，我們還分析了一種比喻類型的存在如何影響另一種。我們的研究結果顯示，無論是人類標註者還是大型語言模型，在混合句子中比在僅含轉喻的句子中更能準確識別轉喻，這表明隱喻的存在使轉喻名詞更為明確。我們的數據集公開可用於：https://github.com/cincynlp/MetFuse。
2604.12919.author: Saptarshi Ghosh et.al.
